This SN specifies hoses for all applications which occur at SMS Siemag. When combined with appropriate hose fittings, these
hoses form hoselines which are further described in the following company standards:
-
Low-pressure hoselines
- SN 756
High-pressure hoselines

2.
Hoses for high-pressure hoselines
2.1
The hoses with wire braid reinforcement or with spiral wire reinforcement are used for high-pressure hydraulic systems
operating on mineral oil basis and with water glycol emulsion. The hoses are also suitable for fuel oils, lubricating oils, water
and air. The operating pressure in air systems is limited to 50 bar.
When used for hydraulic fluids, the hose types are suitable for temperature ranges
from -40°C to +125°C (type 2SN),
from -40°C to + 100°C (types 4SP, 4SH and 6SP).
2.2
Type 2 SN hoses from DN 6 to DN 8 (on the basis of DIN EN 853)
Designation of a hose of type 2 SN for high-pressure hoselines
of nominal diameter DN 8 and a length of 500 mm:
Hose SN 775 - 2 SN 8 x 500
Type 4 SP hoses from DN 12 to DN 20 (on the basis of DIN EN 856)
Type 4 SH hoses from DN 25 to DN 40 (on the basis of DIN EN 856)
Designation of a hose of type 4SP for high-pressure hoselines
of nominal diameter DN 20 and a length of 500 mm:
Hose SN 775 - 4SP 20 x 500
Type 6 SP hoses of DN 50 (on the basis of DIN EN 856)
Designation of a hose of type 6SP for high-pressure hoselines
of nominal diameter DN 50 and a length of 1000 mm:
Hose SN 775 - 6SP 50 x 1000

3.
Hoses for low-pressure hoselines
3.1
The hoses can be used in the low-pressure range for fluids such as lubricating oils, lubricating greases, cooling water,
emulsions, gases (except liquid gas), and air.
When used for liquids, the temperature resistance is between –30 °C and +110 °C.
The hoses are weather-resistant, non-ageing and abrasion-resistant.
3.2
"Gelbring" (hose with yellow ring marks) with non-buckling steel spiral, acc. to TRbF 50, smooth inside and outside.
Temperature range -30°C to +90°C (momentarily up to 110°C). Electric resistance < 106 Ohm, according to DIN EN 12115
and DIN EN 1361.
The inner rubber is of low diffusibility, swellproof, flexible at low temperature, non-discolouring and non-hardening, but sensitive
to ozone. The outer rubber is highly abrasion-resistant and weather-resistant.
Designation of a hose of type N for low-pressure hoselines
of nominal diameter DN 25 and a length of 500 mm:
Hose SN 775 - N 25 x 500

4.
Heat protection hoses
4.1
The heat protection hose offers protection against liquid metal splashes, weld sparks, in short-time exposure to flames and at
high temperatures. The external silicone coating makes the hose resistant to oil, water and many solvents. Lasting protection
at high temperatures can be achieved only with sufficient air volume between the line to be protected and the heat protection
hose.
Thermal resistance:
Permanent exposure:
up to +260 °C
exposure for 15 to 20 minutes
up to +1000 °C
exposure for 15 to 30 seconds up to +1500 °C
4.2
Designation of a heat protection hose of type HSS, size 25
and of a length of 500:
Heat protection hose SN 775 - HSS 25 x 500

5
Hoses for water lines
5.1
The hoses are made of rubber and are used for water lines up to 12 bar maximum pressure.
The temperature range is from 0°C to +50°C.
5.2
Designation of a hose of type W, size 12
and a length of L = 1000 mm:
Hose SN 775 - W 12 x 1000

6
Hoses for compressed-air lines
6.1
The hoses are made of PVC and are used for air lines up to 14 bar maximum pressure.
The temperature range is from 0°C to +50°C.
6.2
Hose type and example of designation
Designation of a hose of type L, size 19
and a length of L = 1000 mm:
Hose SN 775 - L 19 x 1000

sections.章节清洗文本:
This SN specifies hoses for all applications which occur at SMS Siemag. When combined with appropriate hose fittings, these
hoses form hoselines which are further described in the following company standards:
Low-pressure hoselines
- SN 756
High-pressure hoselines

operating on mineral oil basis and with water glycol emulsion. The hoses are also suitable for fuel oils, lubricating oils, water
from -40°C to +125°C (type 2SN),
from -40°C to + 100°C (types 4SP, 4SH and 6SP).
Type 2 SN hoses from DN 6 to DN 8 (on the basis of DIN EN 853)
Designation of a hose of type 2 SN for high-pressure hoselines
Hose SN 775 - 2 SN 8 x 500
Type 4 SP hoses from DN 12 to DN 20 (on the basis of DIN EN 856)
Type 4 SH hoses from DN 25 to DN 40 (on the basis of DIN EN 856)
Designation of a hose of type 4SP for high-pressure hoselines
Hose SN 775 - 4SP 20 x 500
Type 6 SP hoses of DN 50 (on the basis of DIN EN 856)
Designation of a hose of type 6SP for high-pressure hoselines
Hose SN 775 - 6SP 50 x 1000
